appliedUpdates.description: Created .factory/library/gicv3-traps.md documenting two critical GICv3 traps discovered during implementation: (1) ICC_SRE_EL2.Enable must be set in boot.S during EL2→EL1 transition, (2) IGROUPR/GICR_IGROUPR0 must be set to Group 1 NS for interrupt delivery via ICC_IAR1_EL1.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add to GICv3 specifics section: 'ICC_SRE_EL2.Enable=1 must be set during boot.S EL2→EL1 transition before any EL1 ICC register access' and 'All interrupts must be Group 1 NS (GICR_IGROUPR0=0xFFFFFFFF, GICD_IGROUPR[n]=0xFFFFFFFF) for delivery via ICC_IAR1_EL1'.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Feature interrupt-controller-trait-gicv3 worker spent debugging time discovering these requirements. Documented in handoff discoveredIssues but not in AGENTS.md.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Consider adding guidance: 'When migrating hardware abstractions (e.g., interrupt controller), update or remove stale test fixtures in system/test/ that reference the old implementation. DTB test fixtures with old compatible strings are OK (parser tests), but hardware-specific register tests should be updated.'
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Feature interrupt-controller-trait-gicv3 review found stale GICv2 references in interrupt_timer.rs tests (GICC_PMR, GICC_CTLR, ITARGETSR) and device_tree.rs still uses arm,cortex-a15-gic in test fixtures.

rejectedObservations.observation: Skills observation suggesting os-worker skill mention checking IGROUPR and EL2 init requirements
rejectedObservations.reason: Already captured in library (gicv3-traps.md) and recommended for AGENTS.md. Skill-level change is too narrow — these are hardware-specific facts better suited to library/AGENTS.md than skill procedure.
